summary: 2026-06-25 14:42:47 PDT - Re-reviewed only `afbe398...HEAD` and the story notes. The README override-args fix is sound, but the close-flow fix is incomplete: step 4 is now conditional, yet the unchanged hard constraint still mandates offering `re-review or merge` after every fix round.

findings.title: Redesign path still conflicts with mandatory merge fork
findings.claim: The new step 4 correctly says an approved approach/redesign fix makes re-review the only route, but the same file's hard constraints still say, "After fixes, ask \"re-review or merge?\" ... every time." Because that hard constraint remains in force above the new conditional step, the procedure is still internally contradictory and can still lead Claude to offer the forbidden merge option after a redesign, violating AC11.
findings.suggestion: Update the hard constraint to defer to the conditional step-4 fork: for approach/redesign fixes, offer only re-review; otherwise ask `re-review or merge?` and stop for Thomas's answer.
